assertions.text: Recommends pg_atomic (specifically u64 fetch_add) as the primary answer
Cites src/include/port/atomics.h with line numbers (not just file path)
Mentions u64 atomics fall back to spinlock emulation on platforms lacking 8-byte atomicity
Mentions BufferDesc.state as the gold-standard pattern for packed atomic state
Explicitly says 'no lock protects it' should be documented (not omitted)
Mentions barrier requirements only kick in for multi-location ordering and points at README.barrier
Correctly states no lwlocklist.h / wait_event_names.txt update is needed (no tranche)
States the rule as 'acquire in partition-number order'
Gives NUM_BUFFER_PARTITIONS = 128 with file location
Explicitly says LWLocks have NO deadlock detection (system wedges silently)
Notes interrupts/query-cancel are held off while waiting on LWLock (pg_cancel_backend won't help)
Cites storage/buffer/README with line numbers (140-143) for the rule
Cross-references the parallel rule for LockManager partitions and CheckDeadLock at proc.c:1871-1872
Mentions related rules (pin-before-content-lock, release-after-pin, buffer_strategy_lock spinlock)
Identifies all four violations (ereport, CFI, subroutine call, long hold)
Cites spin.h:26-29 specifically for the CHECK_FOR_INTERRUPTS rule
Cites README:8-11 specifically for the 'few dozen instructions / no subroutines' rule
Cites s_lock.c NUM_DELAYS=1000 / PANIC consequence with line range
Names the consequence as cluster PANIC + crash recovery, not just 'hang'
Recommends specifically ProcArrayLock (existing LWLock) for the ProcArray scan
Mentions LWLock auto-release on error (README:23-26) as the key reason it's the right swap
